The staff here were very friendly, professional, and courteous, which made for a welcoming check-in. The room itself was an adequate size and the beds were somewhat comfortable for the stay.

However, the bathroom really needs the most attention. There is no vanity storage space, there was visible mold in the caulk line, and the tub stopper was completely broken. Worst of all, the shower pressure was absolutely abysmal it felt like a weak drizzle of rain rather than an actual shower. Great service from the team, but the bathroom maintenance needs a major upgrade.

This hotel worked out well for us: we each had our own bed, we had a nice view of the city skyline, we were close to food and Bryant Park, and it was pretty clean. There was a lot of construction noise on the street below, which is not the hotel's fault. We slept fine, despite the noise. The beds were firm.

processed_reviews.original_text: Great location! Within walking distance of everything that was important to us and was a decent price for NYC pricing. This location has no in-house breakfast options, but there's a cafe literally right next door so we ended up going there in the morning instead. The coffee in the rooms were dark roast only but they did offer both caffeinated and decaf. Beds/pillows are hit or miss depending on your personal preference. For me, the beds were a bit firmer so after a couple of nights I was starting to get sore. The pillows for me were also too flat so I didn't have much support there either. Doubling up on the pillows was a must to get a decent night's sleep. But for a weekend it was okay. Made my bed at home feel that much more softer when I slept in in again lol.

processed_reviews.original_text: For a weekend business trip where I didn't spend much time in my room at this hotel, it was fine and served me well with the location.  The queen bed room I had on the 8th Floor (804) had some trouble with the door locking which concerned me after having a couple times swiping the key card.  The room looked a bit more fancy in the advertised photos.  The very worn and chipped furniture which made the space a bit cramped and uncomfortable needs to be replaced along with the weird bed lamps with faces on them and the word "Think"...(?) :P  The water pressure was low when I took a shower and the toiletries not so great.  Expected a bit more upscale comfort.  I did sleep okay and had an easy check-out.  The TV and channels available were adequate but the remote was a pain to use.  If location is important to you, this is a good hotel option walking distance to Grand Central and midtown sites like Times Square. Wish there had been more bells & whistles for what I paid.  Nice staff though!

There is a good restaurant right next door and the staff recommended the pizza place across the street which we ended up eating at! All the staff I spoke with were friendly & helpful. Whenever I come back to NYC, I would definitely stay here again!

The hotel is in a great location, next to Bryant Park. The room was fine, no issues. However, the fact that the hotel only had 1 working elevator for a 30 floor hotel is just unacceptable. We were there for 3 days and it was broken before we arrived and still broken when we left and the staff seemed to have no clue when it would be fixed. When you asked them, they would just shrug their shoulders and say it wasn’t their responsibility. It became a total nightmare trying to get back to your room in the afternoon or evening and it was almost impossible trying to go down to leave at any point. We ended up walking down the stairs each time as it was quicker than waiting for an elevator.

Before you make a reservation here, check to see if the elevators are ALL working. If it’s still just 1 elevator in service for 30 floors, find another hotel.

processed_reviews.original_text: There is nothing special about this hotel, really. I am a Titanium Elite member and was not acknowledged at any time during my stay. There is no F&B like every Courtyard is supposed to have. The rooms are dated and have awful (and I mean awful) lighting, even by hotel standards. 1 elevator was broken so the lines to use the elevator were obnoxiously long. Generally, that is ok and I understand that things happen, but the staff had no guidance or training to call the elevator to the lobby on it's downtime so guests would not have to wait. That was just 1 thing on top of all the other things that made the hotel completely forgettable. The housekeeping and maintenance staff were good and very friendly, always making sure they said hello and being as helpful as they could. At checkout, whoever your older gentlemen is working your valet, I would fire him at my hotel immediately. We called an uber to take us to the airport and he berated my wife on why we did that rather than pay the hotel to take us (which the hotel was more.) He wouldn't shut up about it, he brought it up 3 times while we were in the lobby and it was extremely uncomfortable and unprofessional. Overall, for the price point and location, there is absolutely zero reason to stay at this hotel. You can stay at just about any comparable brand hotel in just a block or two radius and get more value for your money, like included breakfast or literally anything for your membership level.

processed_reviews.original_text: I have stayed at the hotel nearly half a dozen times this summer and fall, staying three to four nights each stay. The hotel is located near Bryant Park, and that’s the best reason to stay there. Location, location, location. The staff are helpful. The facility is another story, however. Thankfully, I generally don’t spend very much time in my room. The vent fan in every bathroom is always on. It roars so loudly you have to close the door to muffle the noise to get some sleep. There are two elevators for the 30 story building with a basement. Only one of the two elevators has operated for over a month. There seems to be no sense of urgency to get it fixed. The desk staff have gotten so accustomed to it they don’t bother to apologize to guests for the inconvenience. The rooms are clean, but the furnishings are dated with damaged and chipped drawers and desks throughout. On a scale of A to F, overall the hotel is a weak C.

**Excellent Stay at Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue – 10/10**  

Courtyard New York Manhattan/Fifth Avenue delivers on its promise of inviting, flexible spaces with classic style. The hotel's location at 3 E 40th Street is fantastic—just a block east of Bryant Park and within walking distance of Grand Central Terminal, making it an ideal base for exploring the city. Several metro stations are nearby, providing easy access uptown and downtown.  

The rooms are comfortable, with our two-queen setup providing a great night’s sleep. While the HVAC runs constantly, the lowest setting is 66°F but feels closer to 70°F. The exercise facility, located on the Mezzanine, is compact but well-equipped with Life Fitness machines (a stepper, treadmill, and recumbent bike), two benches, a weight rack, and space for stretching. A convenient coffee machine offering hot water, hot chocolate, and warm milk is also available there.  

Dining is effortless, thanks to **Crema Gourmet**, located right next door. We had breakfast there, and while the food was good, the real advantage was the convenience—perfect for a quick and easy start to the day.  

Service was excellent—Richard, our bellman, arranged a reasonably priced ride to LaGuardia for us. Overall, this hotel is in a prime location, offering a balance of comfort, convenience, and accessibility. I’d definitely stay here again!
